perturbation.original_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder for COMPANY. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its best efforts to provide the following services to COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text: Bravatek agrees to serve as a non-exclusive sales representative for COMPANY. In this capacity, Bravatek will use its best efforts to provide the following services to COMPANY.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'project sales lead finder' to 'sales representative' creates ambiguity. While the original term implied a lead-generation role, 'sales representative' suggests a broader authority in closing deals. This creates uncertainty about Bravatek's actual responsibilities and the scope of its authority. This will contradict section 4, by making a confusion on the compensation that Bravatek will receive
perturbation.contradicted_text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. 2 For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'project sales lead finder' to 'sales representative' introduces a contradiction. The original text clearly defines Bravatek's role as primarily generating leads. The contradicted text then specifies a 'lead-finder fee' structure (10-20% of project revenue). By changing the role to 'sales representative,' the expectation shifts from lead generation to potentially closing deals. This introduces ambiguity regarding which compensation structure applies. As a sales representative the compensation expectations are different from that of a lead finder fee. This creates a strong contradiction with the outlined payment based on being a lead finder, since it is uncertain to know whether or not the compensation applies under this new title.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of five (5) years after termination of this Agreement, the parties shall treat as confidential all information and take every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the same.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of five (5) years after termination of this Agreement, the parties shall treat as restricted all information and take every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential' to 'restricted' introduces ambiguity. 'Confidential information' has a well-established legal meaning. 'Restricted information' is vaguer and could encompass a broader or narrower set of data. This will contradict section 5, by making a confusion that the information has a broader or narrower description than what was originally agreed
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. COMPANY and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate, or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects, the Confidential Information disclosed. The obligation of non-disclosure shall not apply to the following: a. Information at or after such time that is publicly available through no fault of either party b. Information at or after such time that is disclosed to either party by a third party entitled to disclose such information c. Information which is required by law to be disclosed to federal, state or local authorities.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential' to 'restricted' introduces ambiguity regarding the type of information covered. The original text used 'confidential information,' which has a specific legal definition, as elaborated upon in the 'Contradicted Text' which defines 'Confidential Information' in a particular manner. By changing 'confidential' to 'restricted', the scope of information covered by the confidentiality clause becomes unclear, thereby contradicting the clearly defined scope of 'Confidential Information' in the 'Contradicted Text'. This ambiguity directly conflicts with the earlier, specific definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction due to misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may also be cancelled by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'terminated' to 'cancelled' introduces ambiguity. While both imply the ending of the agreement, 'termination' often includes clauses of what happens after the agreement ends. 'Cancellation' does not enforce the prior. This will contradict section 6, by making a confusion on the amount of time that Confidentiality will take place after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of five (5) years after termination of this Agreement, the parties shall treat as confidential all information and take every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the same. The parties agree to take all steps reasonably necessary and appropriate to ensure that their employees, agents, and/or assistants treat all information as confidential and to ensure that such employees, agents, and/or assistants are familiar with and abide by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'terminated' to 'cancelled' introduces ambiguity regarding the post-agreement obligations, specifically concerning the confidentiality period. While both terms indicate the end of the agreement, 'termination' is generally understood to include specific procedures and consequences, while 'cancellation' may not. This ambiguity directly contradicts the provision in the contradicted text that specifies a 'five (5) years after termination' confidentiality period. The change casts doubt on whether the confidentiality clause is applicable if the agreement is merely 'cancelled' rather than 'terminated', thus creating a significant and direct conflict. Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction: the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. This leads to an in-text contradiction.
